name: Chilled Spinach Yogurt Soup
recipeIngredient: 1 ½ to 2 cups vegetable stock or water
2 teaspoons fresh dill (chopped) or 1 teaspoon dried dill
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash and stem the spinach.
2. Peel and chop onion into 1½ cups.
3. Peel and chop potato.
4. Chop dill if using fresh.
5. Heat oil in a pan.
6. Saute onions on low heat for about 10 minutes.
7. Add potatoes, salt, pepper, and stock (or water). Bring to a boil, reduce heat, cover, and simmer for 10 to 15 minutes until potatoes are tender.
8. Add the spinach. When spinach is wilted but still bright green (2 to 3 minutes), turn off the heat.
9. Stir in milk and dill. Blend the mixture in batches until smooth.
10. Whisk in yogurt.
11. Chill about four hours. The soup will thicken as it chills. If too thick, whisk in some milk, stock, or water.
12. Taste and adjust seasoning if necessary.
13. Serve garnished with a grating of nutmeg.
